Next he speaks of the effects of the clouds appearing in the
different directions.
mi MWKMm: trtrnw n^n
mi fer ^ w^yw
If the clouds rise in the east, there will be good crops; in the
south-east, there will be outbreak of fires; in the south, crops will
decay; in the south-west, only partial growth of crops; in the west,
good rains; in the north-west, stormy and sporadic rain; in the north,
very fine and full rain; and in the north-east, bumper crops. The
same effects should be predicted of the wind also arising in the seve-
ral directions.
[The metre again is ^nfvpft,]
trm^TTt ^ or^rr swsprrccm iwn
Fall of meteors, flashes of lightning, thunderbolts, false fires
in the quarters, portentous rumblings in the sky, earthquakes and
the cries of birds and beasts produce the same effects as the clouds
do under the above conditions i.e. directions.
